V2Board.history: V2Board is a Chinese open-source proxy protocol management panel primarily used with V2Ray and similar proxy protocols. It emerged around 2019 as a web interface for managing proxy subscriptions, commonly used to circumvent internet censorship in China.
V2Board.summary_abuse: V2Board carries significant abuse risk. It is a management panel for proxy protocols (V2Ray, Shadowsocks, Trojan) used primarily to circumvent internet censorship. While it has legitimate uses for privacy-conscious users in censored regions, the same infrastructure is frequently used to run commercial VPN-like services that may facilitate anonymous internet access for malicious actors, and it has appeared in cybersecurity research related to proxy abuse and traffic obfuscation services.

v4Guard Checkpoint.history: v4Guard Checkpoint is a website security product by v4Guard that verifies users and blocks access via anonymization services using a CAPTCHA-like widget. No notable ownership changes documented.
v4Guard Checkpoint.summary_abuse: v4Guard Checkpoint has no notable abuse signal. It is a security widget designed to prevent anonymized or fraudulent access, and is not known to be misused by attackers itself.

Vaadin.history: Vaadin was originally created at IT Mill in Finland in the early 2000s as an AJAX UI framework for Java. The company rebranded to Vaadin Ltd around 2010, and the framework became open-source under Apache 2.0. Vaadin has maintained active development through several major version releases (Vaadin 7, 8, 14, and beyond), with a commercial Pro subscription alongside the open-source core.
Vaadin.summary_abuse: Vaadin is a Java/TypeScript web framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a backend-rendered UI toolkit used by enterprise Java developers. There is no consumer-facing surface and no documented use in phishing, malware, or fraudulent campaigns.

Vacation Labs.history: Vacation Labs was founded in 2012 by Shubham Satyarth and Saurabh Nanda in Panaji, India. The company provides a SaaS-based website builder, booking engine, and operational management tools for tour and activity operators worldwide. The platform serves operators in North America, Europe, Africa, and Asia across sectors like scuba diving, trekking, camping, and private tours. The company has raised a modest seed funding round and operates with a small team of 11-50 employees.
Vacation Labs.summary_abuse: Vacation Labs is a tour operator SaaS platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform enables tour operators to create booking websites and manage operations, with no documented cases of abuse for phishing, fraud, or scam hosting. The platform requires merchant setup and is not freely available to anonymous users.

Vagaro.history: Vagaro was founded in 2009 in Dublin, California as an appointment scheduling and business management platform targeting salons, spas, and fitness studios. The company has grown organically and remains independent, serving over 200,000 professionals.
Vagaro.summary_abuse: Vagaro is a scheduling and payment platform for beauty and wellness businesses. It handles PCI-compliant payment processing. No notable abuse patterns have been documented; its customer base is small businesses in the service industry with contractual accounts.

Valuad.history: Valuad is an algorithm-based header bidding platform for publishers to optimize ad revenue; no notable founding date or ownership events are publicly documented.
Valuad.summary_abuse: Valuad is a programmatic advertising header bidding platform. Ad tech platforms can be vectors for malvertising, but no specific documented abuse incidents exist for Valuad specifically.

ValueCommerce.history: ValueCommerce was founded in 1996 in Japan as one of the first affiliate marketing networks in Asia. It operates primarily in the Japanese market and is affiliated with Yahoo Japan, giving it significant reach among Japanese publishers and advertisers.
ValueCommerce.summary_abuse: ValueCommerce is a Japanese affiliate marketing network. Like all affiliate platforms, it can be exploited for cookie stuffing or fraudulent click attribution, but ValueCommerce operates primarily in the regulated Japanese market with established publisher vetting. No major public incidents have been documented.

Vanco Payment Solutions.history: Vanco Payment Solutions was founded in 1999 and specializes in electronic payment processing for nonprofits, churches, and faith-based organizations. It is a niche payments provider focused on recurring giving and donation management.
Vanco Payment Solutions.summary_abuse: Vanco is a payment processor for nonprofits and religious organizations. It is PCI-DSS compliant and operates in a highly regulated space. No notable abuse patterns documented; its B2B focus on vetted nonprofit customers limits consumer-facing risk.

Vanilla.history: Vanilla Forums was created in 2005 by Mark O'Sullivan in Canada as an open-source PHP community forum platform. The company Vanilla Forums Inc. was later formed to offer hosted and enterprise versions. In 2019, Vanilla Forums was acquired by Higher Logic, a community management platform company.
Vanilla.summary_abuse: Vanilla Forums is a community forum platform. Like any community platform, hosted forums can be misused for spam, link farming, or distribution of harmful content if moderation is inadequate. Self-hosted instances have no vendor oversight at all. The vendor provides moderation tools and spam filtering in the hosted version. No notable platform-level abuse incidents have been documented.

Vant.history: Vant is an open-source Vue UI component library for mobile web apps, developed and maintained by the Chinese e-commerce company Youzan (有赞). First released in 2017, it has become one of the most popular mobile Vue UI libraries in China.
Vant.summary_abuse: Vant is a Vue UI component library with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend toolkit used by developers to build mobile web interfaces.

Vanta.history: Vanta was founded in 2018 by Christina Cacioppo and Austin Arensberg in San Francisco. It raised significant VC funding including a $40M Series B in 2021 and a $150M Series C in 2022 at a $1.6B valuation, becoming a unicorn in the compliance automation space. The company integrates with major cloud providers and SaaS tools to continuously monitor security controls.
Vanta.summary_abuse: Vanta is a compliance automation platform that itself holds SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and PCI certifications. It is a security-focused vendor with no notable abuse signal. Its primary risk profile is data access: Vanta requires integrations that grant read access to cloud infrastructure, which represents a supply-chain risk if Vanta were compromised. The vendor has a strong responsible disclosure policy and security posture.

Vantaca.history: Vantaca is a US-based community association management software company serving HOA management companies. It was founded around 2016 and focuses on the property management technology space.
Vantaca.summary_abuse: Vantaca is a niche B2B property management software platform for HOA community managers. It has no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

VAPTCHA.history: VAPTCHA is a Chinese AI-based CAPTCHA and human verification service founded around 2016. It uses gesture-based challenges rather than traditional text/image CAPTCHAs, collecting behavioral data to distinguish humans from bots.
VAPTCHA.summary_abuse: VAPTCHA is a Chinese CAPTCHA service. Like all CAPTCHA solutions embedded on external sites, it collects behavioral data from end users. As a China-based service, data handling practices may not comply with GDPR or other Western privacy regulations. There is no documented pattern of fraud-specific abuse.

Varbase.history: Varbase is a Drupal distribution created by Vardot, a Drupal agency based in Jordan. First released around 2014, it provides a pre-configured Drupal setup with enterprise features including multilingual support, SEO optimization, and content workflows. It is maintained by Vardot as both an open-source community project and an enterprise product.
Varbase.summary_abuse: Varbase is a Drupal distribution with no notable abuse signal. As an open-source CMS framework, self-hosted instances are under the control of site operators. No documented platform-level abuse patterns are associated with Varbase specifically.

Variance.history: Variance Charts is a commercial JavaScript charting library that provides custom data graphics tools using HTML and CSS for web platforms, available at variancecharts.com. The product has a limited public profile and its current development status and company background are not well documented.
Variance.summary_abuse: Variance Charts is a JavaScript data visualization library with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer tool with no consumer-facing surface, and no documented abuse cases or security incidents are associated with the library.

Varify.history: Varify is a German A/B testing and web optimization platform offering flat-rate traffic pricing for conversion optimization. Founded around 2020, it targets German-speaking ecommerce and SaaS companies.
Varify.summary_abuse: Varify is an A/B testing and web optimization tool with no notable abuse signal. Its embedded JavaScript collects visitor behavioral data for conversion testing purposes, which is standard practice in the category. GDPR-compliant deployment is supported given its German origin.

Variti.history: Variti was founded in 2010 and is headquartered in Lucerne, Switzerland, specialising in DDoS protection and active bot mitigation using proprietary real-time traffic analysis and machine learning technology.
Variti.summary_abuse: Variti is a DDoS protection and bot-mitigation security vendor with no notable abuse signal; it operates as a defensive cybersecurity product and is not a surface used by attackers.

Varnish.history: Varnish Cache was created in 2006 by Poul-Henning Kamp, a FreeBSD developer, based on a commission from Norwegian newspaper Verdens Gang (VG). It quickly became the de facto standard HTTP reverse proxy and cache for high-traffic websites. Varnish Software was founded to provide commercial support and enterprise features. The project is BSD-licensed and remains one of the most widely deployed web acceleration technologies.
Varnish.summary_abuse: Varnish Cache is a foundational web caching and reverse proxy technology with no notable abuse signal. It is a backend infrastructure component with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target. Self-hosted deployments are entirely operator-controlled.

Varos.history: Varos was founded in 2020 by Yarden Shaked and Lior Chen in San Francisco. The company emerged from Y Combinator (W21) and raised a $4 million seed round in February 2022 led by Ibex Investors. Varos operates a benchmarking data cooperative for DTC and eCommerce companies, letting brands anonymously share and compare performance metrics like ROAS, CAC, and revenue against industry peers.
Varos.summary_abuse: Varos is a competitive benchmarking analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform aggregates anonymized business performance data from participating companies and has no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing or fraud. No documented abuse cases are associated with the service.

Vaven.history: Vaven is an ad widget creation platform for websites; no notable founding date or ownership events are publicly documented.
Vaven.summary_abuse: Vaven is an ad widget platform. Advertising platforms that allow user-created widgets can potentially be used to serve malvertising, but no specific documented abuse incidents exist for Vaven.

VB Media.history: VB Media is an e-commerce platform specialized in selling print products online; no notable founding date or ownership events are publicly documented.
VB Media.summary_abuse: VB Media is a niche print-product e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal documented.

vBulletin.history: vBulletin was created in 2000 by John Percival and James Limm through Jelsoft Enterprises as a paid PHP forum platform. Internet Brands acquired vBulletin from Jelsoft in 2007. The lead developers left in 2010 to create XenForo as a competitor after a dispute with Internet Brands. vBulletin remains an active product under Internet Brands.
vBulletin.summary_abuse: vBulletin installations are periodically targeted by hackers exploiting known vulnerabilities, with several critical RCE vulnerabilities disclosed in 2019 and later years. Compromised vBulletin forums have been used for spam, credential harvesting, and malware distribution. The software itself is legitimate but the large installed base of legacy deployments creates attack surface.

vChat.history: vChat is a Vietnamese live chat solution for websites, serving primarily the Vietnamese market; no notable ownership changes or deprecation events are documented.
vChat.summary_abuse: vChat is a Vietnamese live chat widget with no notable abuse signal.

vcita.history: vcita was founded in 2010 by Itzik Levy and Eran Utmazgin, initially as a client engagement platform for small service businesses. Headquartered in Seattle with R&D in Tel Aviv, the company has raised venture funding and grown to serve approximately 1.5 million small businesses globally across appointment scheduling, CRM, billing, and marketing features.
vcita.summary_abuse: vcita is a small-business management platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used by legitimate service providers for appointment scheduling and client management. The platform requires business account setup, reducing anonymous signup risk, and does not provide free-tier hosting or custom domain features that are typical of platforms abused for phishing or scam infrastructure.

VClasses.history: VClasses is a subscription LMS platform for setting up online learning environments; no notable founding date or ownership events are publicly documented.
VClasses.summary_abuse: VClasses is a learning management system with no notable abuse signal. LMS platforms serve educational institutions and are not typically targeted for fraud.

VDX.tv.history: VDX.tv (formerly Exponential Interactive) is a global video advertising technology company that rebranded around 2019. It provides video advertising solutions for premium publishers and brands, focusing on connected TV and digital video inventory.
VDX.tv.summary_abuse: VDX.tv is a video advertising technology platform. Like most ad-tech companies, it deploys heavy tracking across its publisher network. Video ad networks have been documented vehicles for malvertising campaigns, and third-party ad tech broadly faces ad fraud and impression-laundering challenges. No specific VDX.tv-attributed incidents are publicly documented.

Ve Global.history: Ve Interactive was founded in 2009 in London as an e-commerce marketing technology company. It was involved in a significant controversy in 2016 when it collapsed into administration following financial difficulties and allegations of fraudulent invoice financing totaling over £100 million. The company was restructured and rebranded as Ve Global.
Ve Global.summary_abuse: Ve Global (formerly Ve Interactive) has a troubled history after its predecessor company collapsed in 2016 amid allegations of invoice fraud affecting investors and creditors. The rebranded entity Ve Global provides ecommerce marketing software. Its ad-tech adjacent nature means it collects visitor behavior data. The legacy financial fraud controversy warrants caution when evaluating vendor trust.

Vectary.history: Vectary was founded in 2014 in Slovakia as a browser-based 3D design and AR tool. It raised seed funding and grew as a web-native alternative to desktop 3D modeling software, serving product designers and web developers who need 3D content for websites and apps.
Vectary.summary_abuse: Vectary is a 3D design and augmented reality creation platform. It has no notable abuse signal; its use case is creative design work with no consumer-facing infrastructure that would attract fraud actors.

Velaro.history: Velaro was founded in 2000 and is headquartered in the United States, with offices in Los Angeles and Baltimore. The company is an independent live chat and customer engagement software provider, serving businesses looking to improve website visitor engagement and online sales conversions. Velaro has operated for over two decades with no notable acquisitions or ownership changes on record.
Velaro.summary_abuse: Velaro is a live chat platform used by businesses for customer support and sales. Like all live chat tools embedded on third-party websites, it has a minor potential for misuse if installed on fraudulent sites, but the platform itself is a B2B product with no documented history of abuse as an attack vector. No notable phishing campaigns, fraud incidents, or security reports are associated with Velaro.

Vello.history: Vello is a Finnish online booking platform for small businesses and service providers. Founded around 2014 in Finland, it provides appointment scheduling software targeting freelancers and small businesses in the Nordic market.
Vello.summary_abuse: Vello is a small business appointment booking platform from Finland. It has no notable abuse signal; it is a scheduling tool with a limited attack surface serving small local service businesses.

Veloce.history: Veloce Solutions is a US-based healthcare CRM and practice management software provider. It targets medical practices seeking patient engagement and operational tools. Limited public information is available about its founding.
Veloce.summary_abuse: Veloce is a healthcare-focused CRM platform serving medical practices. It operates in a regulated industry and has no notable abuse signal. Its B2B focus on credentialed healthcare providers limits consumer-facing risk.

Velocify.history: Velocify was founded in 2004 as a lead management and sales acceleration platform, originally focused on the mortgage and insurance industries. In 2017, Velocify was acquired by Ellie Mae, a leading mortgage technology company. Ellie Mae itself was later acquired by ICE (Intercontinental Exchange) and rebranded to ICE Mortgage Technology, with Velocify now part of that portfolio.
Velocify.summary_abuse: Velocify is a CRM and lead management platform for the mortgage industry, now part of ICE Mortgage Technology. It is a B2B enterprise tool serving regulated financial service companies. No notable abuse signal is associated with Velocify.

Vendaecia.history: Vendaecia (also known as Venda e Cia) is a Brazilian ecommerce platform founded around 2014 by Marcelo Paiva. The company provides a multi-channel ecommerce ecosystem for micro, small, and medium-sized businesses in Brazil, offering dropshipping tools, online store creation, and integrations with Brazilian marketplaces like Mercado Livre, Shopee, and Amazon. The platform focuses exclusively on the Brazilian domestic dropshipping and ecommerce market.
Vendaecia.summary_abuse: Vendaecia is a Brazilian dropshipping and ecommerce platform for small merchants. Dropshipping platforms can be used to set up fraudulent or counterfeit storefronts, and Brazil has a high rate of online consumer fraud. However, no specific documented incidents or security reports are associated with Vendaecia itself. The platform's 'sell first, pay later' model could reduce barriers for fraudulent merchant registrations if KYC controls are weak.

Vendio.history: Vendio was founded in 1999 and is a multi-channel ecommerce management platform for small and medium-sized merchants. In 2006 Vendio acquired Andale, expanding its capabilities. In 2010 Vendio was acquired by Alibaba.com as part of Alibaba's $100 million investment in U.S. ecommerce companies. At its peak the platform served over 80,000 merchants managing nearly $2 billion in annual merchandise sales across channels including eBay, Amazon, and Google.
Vendio.summary_abuse: Vendio is a multi-channel ecommerce management platform with a low abuse signal. The platform is primarily used by legitimate small merchants to manage product listings across marketplaces. As a tool that facilitates selling on third-party marketplaces, it does not host storefronts directly. No documented phishing, fraud, or scam hosting incidents are associated with Vendio.

Vendre.history: Vendre is a modular ecommerce platform allowing organizations to select specific functionality modules. It appears to be a UK-based ecommerce solution with limited public footprint.
Vendre.summary_abuse: Vendre is a niche modular ecommerce system. It has no notable abuse signal and serves organizational buyers who select specific commerce modules.

Venly.history: Venly was founded in 2018 in Belgium as a blockchain wallet and Web3 API provider (originally called Arkane Network). It rebranded to Venly and provides NFT and wallet APIs for developers building Web3 and gaming applications.
Venly.summary_abuse: Venly is a Web3 blockchain wallet and NFT infrastructure provider. The cryptocurrency and NFT space carries elevated risk of scams, token fraud, and rug pulls. Venly's infrastructure can be used by legitimate projects and fraudulent ones alike. The company publishes acceptable use terms but the decentralized nature of blockchain makes enforcement challenging. Users should exercise caution when interacting with Web3 projects using Venly infrastructure.

Venmo.history: Venmo was founded in 2009 by Andrew Kortina and Iqram Magdon-Ismail as a peer-to-peer payment app. In 2012 it was acquired by Braintree, which was itself acquired by PayPal in 2013. Venmo has grown to become one of the most popular P2P payment platforms in the US, with tens of millions of active users.
Venmo.summary_abuse: Venmo is widely abused in scam campaigns due to its social payment features and the irreversibility of completed transactions. Common abuse patterns include romance scams, overpayment scams where fraudsters send more than requested and ask for a partial refund, fake customer support impersonation, and account takeover via phishing. Venmo transactions are public by default, which has also been exploited to harvest financial and social data. PayPal/Venmo has anti-fraud measures and dispute processes but P2P payment reversals are not guaranteed.

Venngage.history: Venngage was founded in 2012 in Toronto, Canada as an online infographic and data visualization design platform. It targets marketing and communications professionals and has grown through content marketing. The platform has added AI-powered design tools in recent years.
Venngage.summary_abuse: Venngage is an infographic and visual design platform. It has no notable abuse signal; it is a creative tool with no significant consumer-facing surface that fraud actors would exploit for phishing or scam operations.

VentasxMayor.history: VentasxMayor is a Spanish-language ecommerce platform targeting wholesalers, distributors, and importers in Latin America. Limited public information is available about its founding and history.
VentasxMayor.summary_abuse: VentasxMayor is a niche B2B wholesale ecommerce platform serving Latin American markets. Like any ecommerce platform, it could be used to set up fraudulent storefronts, but its B2B wholesale focus and limited public footprint suggest low abuse prevalence.

VentraIP.history: VentraIP was founded in 2010 in Australia and has grown to become the largest privately owned web hosting and domain registrar in Australia. It serves hundreds of thousands of Australian customers and remains independent.
VentraIP.summary_abuse: VentraIP is a major Australian web hosting and domain registrar. Like all shared hosting and domain registration services, its infrastructure can be used to host phishing pages or spam sites. VentraIP has an abuse team and AUP, and responds to abuse reports. The abuse risk is typical for a general-purpose web host, not elevated.

Ventrata.history: Ventrata was founded in 2016 in Prague, Czech Republic as a ticketing and booking platform for high-volume tourism attractions, museums, and tour operators. It is designed to handle complex ticketing needs for major attractions.
Ventrata.summary_abuse: Ventrata is an enterprise ticketing platform for tourism attractions. Its B2B model with vetted operator clients limits consumer-facing abuse risk. No notable abuse patterns are documented.

VentryShield.history: VentryShield is a DDoS-protected VPS and web hosting provider; no notable founding date or ownership events are publicly documented.
VentryShield.summary_abuse: VentryShield is a DDoS-protected hosting provider. Small hosting providers with strong DDoS protection can attract abuse from actors seeking bulletproof or hard-to-disrupt infrastructure, though no specific documented abuse pattern is on record for VentryShield.

Venyoo.history: Venyoo is a live chat and callback widget service targeting small and medium businesses; no notable founding date, ownership changes, or acquisitions are publicly documented.
Venyoo.summary_abuse: Venyoo is a live chat and callback widget with no notable abuse signal; it embeds a communication widget on merchant websites and has no anonymous content-hosting surface.

Veonr.history: Veonr is a website analytics and activity tracking SaaS for businesses; no notable founding date or ownership events are publicly documented.
Veonr.summary_abuse: Veonr is a website analytics tool. Analytics platforms that track visitors can raise privacy concerns but Veonr has no specific documented abuse incidents.

Veoxa.history: Veoxa is categorized as an advertising platform; minimal public information is available about its history or founding.
Veoxa.summary_abuse: Veoxa has no notable abuse signal based on available information. It appears to be a niche advertising platform.

Vepaar.history: Vepaar is an Indian SaaS startup offering WhatsApp CRM, online store, and messaging tools for small businesses. Founded around 2020, it targets small business owners in South Asia who use WhatsApp for business communication.
Vepaar.summary_abuse: Vepaar is a WhatsApp CRM and online store platform for small businesses. WhatsApp-based CRM tools can be used for spam campaigns or unsolicited business messaging. No specific documented abuse incidents are associated with Vepaar. The free tier with WhatsApp messaging capabilities warrants attention for potential spam use.

Vera.history: Vera (formerly Privasee) is a UK-based GDPR self-compliance tool for SMEs rebranded to getvera.ai. It helps small businesses understand their data processing activities and demonstrate GDPR compliance without a dedicated privacy professional.
Vera.summary_abuse: Vera is a GDPR compliance tool designed to help businesses manage data privacy. It has no notable abuse signal; its purpose is to improve privacy compliance, and it presents no consumer-facing surface that fraud actors would target.

VeraCore.history: VeraCore is a US-based order fulfillment software platform providing inventory management and warehousing tools for distributors and fulfillment providers. It has served the fulfillment industry for over two decades.
VeraCore.summary_abuse: VeraCore is a B2B fulfillment software with no documented abuse cases.

VeraSafe.history: VeraSafe was founded in 2012 in Washington DC as a privacy law firm and compliance technology provider. It specializes in transatlantic data privacy compliance, GDPR, and US privacy regulations. VeraSafe is an EU-US Privacy Shield and EU-representative certified body.
VeraSafe.summary_abuse: VeraSafe is a privacy compliance platform and legal services firm. Its core product helps organizations comply with GDPR and global privacy laws. No notable abuse signal is associated with VeraSafe.

Vercel.history: Vercel was founded in 2015 by Guillermo Rauch (also creator of Next.js) as ZEIT, and rebranded to Vercel in 2020. It has raised over 00M in venture funding and is the primary hosting and deployment platform for Next.js. It popularized the JAMstack deployment model and serverless edge computing for frontend teams.
Vercel.summary_abuse: Vercel is a mainstream infrastructure platform. Its free tier with custom domain support makes it an occasional target for phishing page hosting, similar to Netlify and GitHub Pages. Vercel publishes abuse reporting mechanisms and removes violating deployments, but automated deployments via CI pipelines can create short-lived abuse windows.

Vercel Analytics.history: Vercel Analytics is a privacy-focused web analytics product built into the Vercel platform, launched around 2022. It provides real user monitoring and audience insights for websites deployed on Vercel.
Vercel Analytics.summary_abuse: Vercel Analytics is a B2B analytics product tied to the Vercel deployment platform with no documented abuse cases.

Vercel Speed Insights.history: Vercel Speed Insights is a Core Web Vitals and performance monitoring tool built into the Vercel platform, introduced around 2023. It tracks real user metrics for websites deployed on Vercel.
Vercel Speed Insights.summary_abuse: Vercel Speed Insights is a performance monitoring product with no documented abuse cases.

Verfacto.history: Verfacto is an ecommerce marketing analytics and customer segmentation platform; it integrates with Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, and other platforms, and made its analytics tier free in 2021.
Verfacto.summary_abuse: Verfacto is an ecommerce analytics and personalisation tool with no notable abuse signal; it collects behavioural data on behalf of merchants and has no anonymous content-hosting or public-facing surface.

VergeCloud.history: VergeCloud was incorporated in India in October 2024 by Hamid Rostami and Amin Habibi; it raised $3M and rapidly expanded to 40+ global PoPs, positioning itself as India's first globally trusted CDN and edge platform.
VergeCloud.summary_abuse: VergeCloud is a nascent CDN and DDoS-protection infrastructure provider; like all CDN operators it carries inherent risk of abusive tenants misusing its network, but no specific incidents are documented and it is too early-stage to have an established abuse profile.

Vergic.history: Vergic was founded in Sweden in 2007 by Alexander Lunde, Fredrik Lunde, and Johan Ringsberg to provide digital customer engagement software; in October 2021 it was acquired by Puzzel, a Nordic contact-centre platform.
Vergic.summary_abuse: Vergic is a digital customer engagement and live-chat platform with no notable abuse signal; it serves enterprise clients in financial services and retail with no anonymous content-hosting surface.

Verifone 2Checkout.history: 2Checkout was founded in 2000 as an online payment gateway and acquired by Avangate in 2017. Verifone, the payment terminal manufacturer, acquired Avangate in 2018 and operates the platform as Verifone 2Checkout. It serves global merchants with digital goods and subscription billing.
Verifone 2Checkout.summary_abuse: Verifone 2Checkout is a payment processor with no documented specific abuse incidents beyond the standard fraud risk inherent to payment processing. PCI-DSS compliance provides baseline security assurance.

VerifyPass.history: VerifyPass was founded in 2020 in Paramus, New Jersey as an identity verification and community authentication platform offering gated discounts for specific groups such as seniors and military personnel.
VerifyPass.summary_abuse: VerifyPass is an identity and community-verification widget with no notable abuse signal; it gates promotional offers to verified groups and includes KYC checks that limit fraudulent use.

VerifyPress.history: VerifyPress is a social proof notification SaaS enabling pop-up notifications and HelloBar alerts on websites; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
VerifyPress.summary_abuse: VerifyPress is a social proof and notification tool. These tools display pop-ups claiming recent user activity and can be used deceptively to show false social proof, but there is no specific documented abuse record for VerifyPress.

Verisign.history: Verisign was founded in 1995 as a spin-off from RSA Security to provide internet trust services including SSL certificates and domain name registry. It became the authoritative registry operator for .com and .net TLDs. Over time Verisign divested many business units and is now a public company (NASDAQ: VRSN) focused exclusively on domain name registry and internet security.
Verisign.summary_abuse: Verisign operates critical internet infrastructure — the .com and .net domain registries. While domains registered through Verisign-managed TLDs can be used for phishing or spam, this is a registrar-level problem rather than Verisign's direct service. Verisign participates in ICANN abuse-mitigation processes and can suspend domains under its registry agreements. Its direct service surface has no notable abuse pattern.

Verizon Media.history: Verizon Media was created in 2015 when Verizon acquired AOL, and later rebranded after also acquiring Yahoo in 2017. In 2021, Verizon sold the division (AOL and Yahoo assets) to Apollo Global Management for $5 billion, and it was rebranded as Yahoo Inc. The advertising platform is now operated under the Yahoo brand.
Verizon Media.summary_abuse: Verizon Media (now Yahoo Advertising) is a large ad tech and media platform. As an ad network serving billions of impressions, it has historically been involved in malvertising incidents common to large ad networks. It maintains an AUP and abuse reporting processes, but its scale means ad-delivered malware and fraudulent advertisers are documented risks across the industry.

Verloop.history: Verloop was founded around 2016 in India as an AI-powered customer support automation platform, primarily targeting enterprises in Southeast Asia and India; no notable ownership changes documented.
Verloop.summary_abuse: Verloop is a B2B chatbot and customer support platform; it has no notable abuse signal as access requires enterprise onboarding with no anonymous or consumer-facing signup.

Vero.history: Vero was founded around 2012 as a behavioural email marketing platform; it has remained independent and focused on event-triggered email campaigns, with no notable acquisitions or ownership changes.
Vero.summary_abuse: Vero is a transactional and behavioural email platform. Like all bulk email sending services, it can be misused to send spam or phishing emails, but its B2B onboarding process and usage-based pricing deter low-effort abuse. No documented large-scale abuse incidents are known.

Versa Commerce.history: VersaCommerce is a German e-commerce SaaS platform founded around 2012, offering online shop and POS capabilities primarily to small German-speaking businesses; no notable ownership changes documented.
Versa Commerce.summary_abuse: VersaCommerce is a legitimate German e-commerce platform targeting SMBs; it has no notable abuse signal. Like any e-commerce enabler it could be used to run fraudulent storefronts, but this is typical industry risk with no documented platform-level issues.

VersaTailor.history: VersaTailor is a web analytics tool for online businesses with hourly filtering capabilities; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
VersaTailor.summary_abuse: VersaTailor is a B2B web analytics tool with no notable abuse signal documented.

VerseOne.history: VerseOne is a UK-based CMS vendor specializing in the healthcare and housing sectors; it has operated since the mid-2000s with no notable ownership changes or acquisitions documented.
VerseOne.summary_abuse: VerseOne is a sector-specific CMS for healthcare and housing organisations; it has no notable abuse signal given its specialist B2B focus and controlled deployment model.

Vertex.history: Vertex is a free WordPress theme by DessignThemes, designed for portfolio and photography sites; it is a simple theme with no notable ownership or development history beyond being a basic free template offering.
Vertex.summary_abuse: Vertex is a WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal; it is a static website theme with no consumer-facing service or content-hosting surface that fraudsters would target.

VerticalScope.history: VerticalScope is a Canadian online community platform founded around 2006 that owns and operates a large network of niche forums and discussion communities focused on hobbies (cars, sports, pets) monetized through display advertising; it went public on the Toronto Stock Exchange in 2021.
VerticalScope.summary_abuse: VerticalScope hosts user-generated content across hundreds of niche forums, which creates a moderate spam and account-abuse surface. Forum networks of this type are commonly targeted by spammers registering accounts to post links. The platform is ad-supported and collects significant user-behaviour data, but is not primarily associated with fraud or phishing infrastructure.

Vertster.history: Vertster is a multivariate A/B testing SaaS; it is a small niche platform with no notable ownership changes or documented history beyond its core product offering.
Vertster.summary_abuse: Vertster is a website A/B testing platform with no notable abuse signal; it is a backend analytics tool with no consumer-facing or content-hosting surface.

Very Good Security.history: Very Good Security (VGS) was founded in 2015 in San Francisco to provide data vault and tokenisation infrastructure for handling sensitive PII and payment data without developers touching it directly. It has raised over $90M in venture funding and targets fintech and payments companies.
Very Good Security.summary_abuse: VGS is a data security and tokenisation platform designed specifically to reduce sensitive data exposure; it has no notable abuse signal. Its core purpose is preventing data breaches rather than enabling abuse, and its enterprise B2B model does not expose a consumer-facing surface.

Vestico.history: Vestico is a digital asset management (DAM) SaaS for organizing and distributing digital content; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Vestico.summary_abuse: Vestico is a digital asset management platform with no notable abuse signal. DAM systems are B2B tools used by marketing teams and do not expose a consumer-facing content surface.

Vev.history: Vev is a Norwegian cloud-based interactive design and publishing platform founded around 2017, targeting digital content teams and agencies; it has raised venture funding and remained independent.
Vev.summary_abuse: Vev is a web publishing and design tool with a free tier. Like other page-builder platforms, it could potentially host phishing or scam landing pages, but it is not known as a preferred abuse vector and requires user registration to publish content.

Vex.history: Vex is a lightweight vanilla JavaScript dialog library. It was created around 2013 and has been largely unmaintained in recent years.
Vex.summary_abuse: Vex is a small client-side dialog library with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

Vextras.history: Vextras is a small SaaS platform providing marketing and support add-ons for WooCommerce stores; no notable ownership history is publicly documented.
Vextras.summary_abuse: Vextras is a WooCommerce add-on platform with no notable abuse signal; it extends existing e-commerce shops and has no standalone content-hosting or anonymous signup surface.

vFairs.history: vFairs was founded in 2016 by Muhammad Younas and Arfeen Godil as a virtual and hybrid event management platform. The company is headquartered in Coppell, Texas with the majority of its workforce in Pakistan. It experienced 20x revenue growth in 2020 during the COVID-19 pandemic as organizations rapidly adopted virtual event formats.
vFairs.summary_abuse: vFairs is an event management and virtual events platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used by enterprises and organizations to run trade shows, career fairs, and conferences. There is no consumer-facing open-access platform or free tier that would attract scammers.

Vfinder.history: Vfinder is an e-commerce product coordination and recommendation display platform; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Vfinder.summary_abuse: Vfinder is an e-commerce upsell and recommendation tool with no notable abuse signal documented.

ViaBill.history: ViaBill is a Danish buy-now-pay-later payment solution founded around 2013, primarily targeting e-commerce merchants in Denmark and the US; it remains independent with no major acquisitions.
ViaBill.summary_abuse: ViaBill is a buy-now-pay-later payment service. BNPL schemes can be exploited for payment fraud and friendly-fraud chargebacks, and BNPL providers generally face higher fraud rates than card payments. ViaBill's exposure is proportional to that industry risk, with no documented platform-specific abuse campaigns.

Viafoura.history: Viafoura is a Canadian audience engagement platform founded around 2012, providing commenting, live blogs, and social interaction tools for media publishers; it has raised venture funding and remained independent.
Viafoura.summary_abuse: Viafoura provides audience engagement and commenting tools embedded on media publisher sites. User-generated comment sections can attract spam and harassment, and the platform must manage moderation at scale. No documented platform-level exploitation for phishing or malware is known.

ViArt Shop.history: ViArt Shop is a PHP-based e-commerce shopping cart and CMS solution that has been available since the early 2000s; it is a self-hosted licensed product targeting small merchants and has had no notable ownership changes.
ViArt Shop.summary_abuse: ViArt Shop is a self-hosted e-commerce package; like any store software it can be installed to run fraudulent storefronts, but the platform itself has no notable abuse signal and requires manual installation on a web host.

ViaSay.history: ViaSay is a French conversational AI and chatbot platform, previously known as Mindsay, targeting customer service automation for enterprises; it rebranded to ViaSay around 2021.
ViaSay.summary_abuse: ViaSay is a B2B chatbot and conversational AI platform with no notable abuse signal; its enterprise deployment model with dedicated onboarding does not expose a consumer-facing anonymous surface.

vibecommerce.history: vibecommerce is a Brazilian ecommerce platform operating at vibecommerce.com.br. Very little public information is available about its founding date or history.
vibecommerce.summary_abuse: vibecommerce is a small Brazilian ecommerce platform with no documented abuse signal in public sources. As a regional SMB ecommerce solution it has limited exposure.

Vicomi.history: Vicomi is an Israeli content analytics and audience reaction tool founded around 2013; it provides sentiment and reaction widgets embeddable on publisher websites with no notable ownership changes.
Vicomi.summary_abuse: Vicomi is an audience analytics widget embedded on content sites; it collects user reaction data and sends it to Vicomi servers, creating a cross-site tracking profile. No phishing or active abuse patterns are documented.

Vidazoo.history: Vidazoo is an Israeli video content and yield management platform founded around 2016, targeting digital publishers with video monetisation tools; it has raised venture funding.
Vidazoo.summary_abuse: Vidazoo is a video ad yield management platform embedded on publisher sites. Video ad networks have historically been vectors for malvertising and domain spoofing. Vidazoo's ad-tech orientation means its code ships heavy tracking and ad scripts, but no specific large-scale abuse incidents are documented.

Video Greet.history: Video Greet is a Shopify app that lets customers attach QR-code video messages to gift purchases; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Video Greet.summary_abuse: Video Greet is a niche gift video messaging app with no notable abuse signal. It operates within Shopify's app ecosystem.

VideoJS.history: Video.js was created in 2010 by Steve Heffernan as an open-source HTML5 video player. It became one of the most widely used video players on the web. Brightcove, a video platform company, became the primary sponsor and maintainer of the project.
VideoJS.summary_abuse: Video.js is a foundational open-source video player library with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

Videoly.history: Videoly is a Finnish SaaS platform founded around 2015 that automatically matches and embeds product video reviews on e-commerce product pages; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Videoly.summary_abuse: Videoly is an e-commerce video integration tool with no notable abuse signal; it embeds pre-approved video content on retail pages and does not allow arbitrary user content uploads.

Videoo.tv.history: Videoo.tv is a video platform for enhancing website performance through video content delivery; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Videoo.tv.summary_abuse: Videoo.tv is a niche video delivery and website enhancement SaaS with no notable abuse signal documented.

VideoPal.history: VideoPal is an interactive avatar widget for websites providing interactive help and engagement; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
VideoPal.summary_abuse: VideoPal is an interactive avatar widget tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B embed-based tool without user-hosted content capabilities.

Vidjet.history: Vidjet is a French shoppable video SaaS platform founded around 2021, targeting e-commerce stores by enabling interactive shoppable videos; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Vidjet.summary_abuse: Vidjet is a shoppable video tool for e-commerce with no notable abuse signal; it requires merchant onboarding and does not host arbitrary user content.

Vidora.history: Vidora is a US-based ML platform for consumer data and behavioural prediction, founded around 2014; it targets media and e-commerce companies with real-time personalisation pipelines and has raised venture funding.
Vidora.summary_abuse: Vidora is a machine learning personalisation platform processing consumer behavioural data; it is ad-tech adjacent with data aggregation capabilities, but operates as a B2B enterprise service with no direct consumer surface or documented abuse patterns.

Vidscrip.history: Vidscrip is a US-based SaaS platform enabling physicians to send personalised video messages to patients; it is a healthcare communication niche tool with no notable ownership changes.
Vidscrip.summary_abuse: Vidscrip is a healthcare video messaging tool for clinician-patient communication; it has no notable abuse signal and requires medical provider credentialing with no anonymous consumer access.

Vigbo.history: Vigbo is a Ukrainian website builder platform founded around 2013, targeting photographers and portfolio creators; it offers hosting and website management tools and has remained independent.
Vigbo.summary_abuse: Vigbo is a website builder with hosted plans. Like other SaaS website builders with free or low-cost plans, it could be used to host fraudulent or phishing pages, though no documented abuse campaigns have been reported for this specific platform.

Vigil.history: Vigil is an open-source Rust-based microservices status page tool created around 2018; it is a self-hosted solution for monitoring distributed infrastructure and has remained an independent solo-maintained project.
Vigil.summary_abuse: Vigil is a self-hosted OSS status page tool with no notable abuse signal; it has no cloud-hosted consumer surface and is deployed entirely within the user's own infrastructure.

Vignette.history: Vignette was a major enterprise CMS vendor founded in 1995 in Austin, Texas. It was acquired by Open Text Corporation in 2009, and the product was gradually folded into OpenText's Web Experience Management suite. The Vignette brand has been effectively deprecated.
Vignette.summary_abuse: Vignette is a legacy enterprise CMS now part of OpenText with no notable abuse signal; it is an enterprise on-premise system with no consumer-facing surface and is largely deprecated.

VikaOn.history: VikaOn is a Turkish e-commerce platform enabling businesses and consumers to create and manage online stores; no notable ownership changes are documented.
VikaOn.summary_abuse: VikaOn is a Turkish e-commerce platform with no specific abuse signal documented; like any e-commerce enabler it could be used to run fraudulent storefronts, but no platform-level abuse patterns are known.

Vike.history: Vike is an open-source framework for adding SSR and SSG capabilities to Vite-based applications; it is maintained primarily as a community OSS project with active development since around 2021.
Vike.summary_abuse: Vike is an open-source Vite framework extension with no abuse signal. It is a developer tool with no consumer-facing surface.

Vikreta.history: Vikreta is an open-source web storefront and POS solution built with React, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS; it is a relatively new project with limited public history.
Vikreta.summary_abuse: Vikreta is an open-source e-commerce framework with no abuse signal. As a developer tool for building storefronts, the abuse surface is on the deployments made by users, not the framework itself.

Viloud.history: Viloud was founded in 2015 in Alicante, Spain as an online video platform for creating and distributing linear and on-demand TV channels; no notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Viloud.summary_abuse: Viloud is a video channel creation platform with no notable abuse signal; it serves brands and publishers creating legitimate online TV channels and has no anonymous public-upload surface.

Vimeo.history: Vimeo was founded in 2004 by Jake Lodwick and Zach Klein as a video sharing platform focused on quality creative content. It became an independent public company in 2021 when it was spun off from IAC. Vimeo has positioned itself as a creator-focused, ad-free alternative to YouTube.
Vimeo.summary_abuse: Vimeo hosts user-uploaded video content with a free tier, which creates some abuse surface for hosting malicious or misleading video content. However, Vimeo has a content moderation team, publishes guidelines, and responds to DMCA and abuse reports. Its reputation for creative and professional content means abuse is a small fraction of total use.

Vimeo OTT.history: Vimeo OTT (formerly VHX) was acquired by Vimeo in 2016 to allow creators and brands to launch white-label subscription video services. It is now fully integrated as Vimeo's OTT offering.
Vimeo OTT.summary_abuse: Vimeo OTT is a subscription video platform for creators; it has limited abuse surface since each channel is separately managed by a subscribing creator and requires payment setup. No notable platform-specific abuse campaigns are documented.

Vimos.history: Vimos is a marketing automation SaaS; no notable founding date or ownership events are publicly documented.
Vimos.summary_abuse: Vimos is a marketing automation platform. Marketing automation tools can be misused for spam campaigns but Vimos has no specific documented abuse incidents.

Vincere.history: Vincere is a Singapore-based recruitment software platform founded in 2012, providing CRM, ATS, and business intelligence tools for staffing and recruitment agencies; it has raised funding and remained independent.
Vincere.summary_abuse: Vincere is a B2B recruitment agency software platform with no notable abuse signal; it requires enterprise account setup and has no anonymous or consumer-facing surface.

vinSUITE.history: vinSUITE was founded in 2003 as a software platform specifically for US wineries providing direct-to-consumer ecommerce, wine club management, tasting room POS, and CRM in one system. The company was acquired by Constellation Software, a Canadian publicly-traded acquirer of vertical-market software businesses.
vinSUITE.summary_abuse: vinSUITE is a niche DTC software platform for wineries with no notable abuse signal. It processes wine sales and club memberships for licensed wineries, which are regulated businesses. There is no consumer-facing open platform that could be exploited for phishing or fraud.

Vintcer.history: Vintcer is a drag-and-drop website builder with hosting and email marketing; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Vintcer.summary_abuse: Vintcer is a website builder with hosting. Website builders that offer free hosting can be used to create phishing pages, but Vintcer is a small niche provider and no specific documented abuse incidents are on record.

Vioma.history: Vioma is a German online marketing system targeting hospitality and tourism businesses; it has operated since the early 2000s with no notable ownership changes documented.
Vioma.summary_abuse: Vioma is a marketing system for hospitality businesses with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B tool for travel and hotel merchants with no anonymous consumer-facing surface.

Viqeo.history: Viqeo is a short video platform for media and e-commerce publishers, founded around 2017 in Russia; it offers embeddable video widgets and has no notable ownership changes documented.
Viqeo.summary_abuse: Viqeo is a video platform for publishers with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B tool requiring merchant integration and does not host arbitrary user-generated content.

Viral Loops.history: Viral Loops is a Greek-founded referral and viral marketing SaaS founded in 2017; it provides campaign templates for referral programs modelled on successful campaigns like Dropbox and Airbnb.
Viral Loops.summary_abuse: Viral Loops is a referral marketing platform; referral programs can be exploited for reward fraud (fake referrals, account farming), and this is an industry-wide risk for such tools. No documented platform-specific exploits are known.

Virgool.history: Virgool is an Iranian blogging and content publishing platform founded around 2017, popular in the Persian-speaking community as a Medium-like service; it operates primarily in Iran.
Virgool.summary_abuse: Virgool is an Iranian blogging platform with user-generated content. As an open publishing platform it has some spam and low-quality content risk, but no documented large-scale abuse campaigns targeting it for phishing or malware distribution.

VirtFusion.history: VirtFusion is a virtualization management platform for hosting providers, founded around 2018; it enables hosting companies to provision and manage KVM virtual machines for their customers.
VirtFusion.summary_abuse: VirtFusion is a VPS management platform for hosting providers. While VPS hosting infrastructure is broadly used for various legitimate and illegitimate purposes, VirtFusion itself is the management layer used by reputable hosting providers. Abuse depends on the policies of the hosting company deploying it.

Virtooal.history: Virtooal is an Israeli augmented reality virtual try-on platform for cosmetics, eyewear, and accessories in e-commerce; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Virtooal.summary_abuse: Virtooal is an e-commerce AR try-on widget with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B product requiring merchant integration with no direct consumer-facing standalone surface.

Virtuagym.history: Virtuagym is a Dutch cloud-based fitness and gym management platform founded in 2010; it provides membership management, coaching, and scheduling tools for gyms and health clubs. It has raised venture funding and remained independent.
Virtuagym.summary_abuse: Virtuagym is a fitness club management SaaS with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B platform for gyms with no anonymous or consumer-facing content-hosting surface.

Virtual Chat.history: Virtual Chat is an Israeli live-chat service for websites; no notable founding details, ownership changes, or acquisitions are publicly documented.
Virtual Chat.summary_abuse: Virtual Chat is a live-chat widget with no notable abuse signal; it provides operator-mediated chat support and has no anonymous content-hosting surface.

VirtualSpirits.history: VirtualSpirits is an Israeli chatbot and live-chat platform for websites, founded around 2012; it provides automated chatbots alongside live agent handoff and has no notable ownership changes.
VirtualSpirits.summary_abuse: VirtualSpirits is a chatbot and live-chat service with no notable abuse signal; it requires merchant integration and does not provide an anonymous consumer-facing communication surface.

VirtueMart.history: VirtueMart is an open-source e-commerce extension for Joomla, first released in 2004. It was originally based on mambo-phpShop and rewritten as VirtueMart 1.0 in 2006. It remains one of the most widely used e-commerce plugins for the Joomla CMS.
VirtueMart.summary_abuse: VirtueMart is a self-hosted Joomla e-commerce extension. Like any e-commerce software it can be installed to run fraudulent storefronts, but this is at the discretion of the site owner rather than a platform-level issue. Outdated installations are sometimes exploited through known vulnerabilities, as is common for legacy PHP software.

Virtuoso.history: Virtuoso (from OpenLink Software) is a multi-model RDF and relational database engine first released in the late 1980s. It powers the DBpedia knowledge graph and is widely used in semantic web and linked data applications. OpenLink Software has maintained it for decades.
Virtuoso.summary_abuse: Virtuoso is a database engine used by semantic web and linked data applications; it has no notable abuse signal as it is a backend infrastructure component with no consumer-facing surface.

Virtuous.history: Virtuous is a US-based fundraising and donor management platform founded around 2014, targeting nonprofit organisations; it has raised venture funding and focuses on responsive fundraising practices.
Virtuous.summary_abuse: Virtuous is a nonprofit CRM and fundraising platform with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B product for registered nonprofits with no anonymous or consumer-facing content surface.

Virtusize.history: Virtusize is a Swedish-Japanese e-commerce personalisation company founded in 2011, providing size recommendation and fit technology for fashion retailers; it has offices in Stockholm and Tokyo and serves major fashion brands.
Virtusize.summary_abuse: Virtusize is a B2B size recommendation widget for fashion retailers with no notable abuse signal; it does not host user content and requires merchant integration.

Visa.history: Visa was founded in 1958 by Bank of America as BankAmericard before becoming VisaNet and eventually Visa Inc. It went public on the NYSE in 2008. Today it operates the world's largest retail electronic payments network, processing billions of transactions annually.
Visa.summary_abuse: Visa is critical payment infrastructure and is a target for card-not-present fraud, card skimming, and account takeover at the issuer/acquirer level rather than at Visa itself. Visa operates extensive anti-fraud systems including Visa Secure and publishes PCI DSS compliance frameworks. Fraudsters exploit the Visa network through compromised merchants and stolen card data, not through Visa's own systems.

Visa Checkout.history: Visa Checkout was Visa's one-click digital wallet solution launched in 2014. Visa announced it would be replaced by Visa's Click to Pay (aligned with EMV SRC standard) starting in 2019, and Visa Checkout has been deprecated in favour of the industry-standard Click to Pay.
Visa Checkout.summary_abuse: Visa Checkout is a deprecated digital wallet service from Visa; it had no notable abuse signal beyond the general payment fraud risks common to all digital payment services, which Visa's fraud detection systems address.

VisBook.history: VisBook is a Norwegian property management system for hotels and accommodation providers, founded around 1998; it serves the Nordic hospitality market and has remained independent.
VisBook.summary_abuse: VisBook is a property management SaaS for hotels with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B system for accommodation providers with no consumer-facing public content surface.

Visely.history: Visely is a product recommendation app for Shopify stores, founded around 2016; it provides AI-driven product personalisation for e-commerce merchants with no notable ownership changes.
Visely.summary_abuse: Visely is a Shopify product recommendation widget with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B tool requiring merchant integration with no consumer-facing standalone surface.

Visenze.history: ViSenze is a Singaporean AI commerce platform founded in 2012, specialising in visual search and product discovery for e-commerce; it has raised significant venture funding and serves major global retailers.
Visenze.summary_abuse: ViSenze is a B2B AI search platform for retailers with no notable abuse signal; it processes product images for merchants and does not expose a consumer-facing content-hosting surface.

Visible Privacy.history: Visible Privacy is a consent management platform for GDPR compliance, compatible with major CMS and e-commerce platforms; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Visible Privacy.summary_abuse: Visible Privacy is a GDPR consent management tool with no notable abuse signal. CMP tools are used to implement privacy compliance rather than as abuse vectors.

VisiOpt.history: VisiOpt is an AI-driven A/B testing and CRO optimization platform; no notable founding details, ownership changes, or acquisitions are publicly documented.
VisiOpt.summary_abuse: VisiOpt is an A/B testing and funnel optimisation tool with no notable abuse signal; it serves digital marketers and has no anonymous content-hosting surface.

Visitor Chat.history: Visitor Chat is a managed live-chat service providing human agents for automotive and other industry websites; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Visitor Chat.summary_abuse: Visitor Chat is a managed live-chat service with no notable abuse signal; it provides human agent conversations for business websites and does not expose an anonymous consumer surface.

Visitor.js.history: Visitor.js is a website personalization tool for customizing content based on user behavior; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Visitor.js.summary_abuse: Visitor.js is a personalization tool. Website personalization scripts can raise privacy concerns but Visitor.js has no specific documented abuse incidents.

VisitorVille.history: VisitorVille is a website analytics tool that visualises web traffic as a SimCity-style cityscape; it was one of the early creative analytics visualisation tools and has seen declining activity in recent years.
VisitorVille.summary_abuse: VisitorVille is a web analytics visualisation tool with no notable abuse signal; it is a read-only analytics service with no consumer-facing or content-hosting surface.

Viskan.history: Viskan is a Swedish scalable e-commerce platform, founded around 2000, targeting mid-market and enterprise retailers in the Nordic region; it has remained independent.
Viskan.summary_abuse: Viskan is a B2B e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal; it serves enterprise retailers and requires substantial integration with no anonymous consumer content surface.

Visual Composer.history: Visual Composer is a WordPress page builder plugin originally created by WPBakery as WPBakery Page Builder (formerly VC). It later split into two separate products: WPBakery Page Builder and Visual Composer Website Builder, the latter developed by the Latvian company Visual Composer. Both remain popular WordPress page builders.
Visual Composer.summary_abuse: Visual Composer is a WordPress page builder plugin with no specific abuse signal; as a frontend design tool for WordPress sites it does not introduce unique abuse vectors beyond those inherent in WordPress installations generally.

Visual Portfolio.history: Visual Portfolio is a WordPress gallery plugin by nK (a Ukrainian developer), available on WordPress.org with free and pro versions; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Visual Portfolio.summary_abuse: Visual Portfolio is a WordPress gallery plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted tool that does not expose a consumer content hosting surface.

Visual Quiz Builder.history: Visual Quiz Builder is a Shopify quiz app developed by AskWhai; no notable ownership changes or founding date are publicly documented.
Visual Quiz Builder.summary_abuse: Visual Quiz Builder is a Shopify quiz tool with no notable abuse signal. It operates within the Shopify ecosystem.

Visualsoft.history: Visualsoft is a UK-based e-commerce platform and agency founded in 1998, providing web design, development, and digital marketing services for online retailers; it has remained independent.
Visualsoft.summary_abuse: Visualsoft is a UK e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal; it serves established retailers under enterprise contracts with no anonymous consumer content surface.

Visx.history: Visx is a collection of React-based data visualization primitives developed by Airbnb and open-sourced around 2020. It provides low-level building blocks for creating custom visualizations with D3 and React.
Visx.summary_abuse: Visx is a client-side data visualization library with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

Vitals.history: Vitals is a Shopify all-in-one marketing app from Portugal offering over 40 marketing features in a single app; it has grown to become one of the top-rated apps in the Shopify app store.
Vitals.summary_abuse: Vitals is a legitimate Shopify marketing app with no notable abuse signal. It operates within Shopify's app ecosystem with its own review and abuse policies.

Vite.history: Vite was created by Evan You (creator of Vue.js) and first released in 2020. It quickly became the dominant frontend build tool, replacing webpack-based setups for many projects. It is backed by the Vue/Vite ecosystem and has received broad community adoption across React, Vue, Svelte, and other frameworks.
Vite.summary_abuse: Vite is a frontend build tool and has no notable abuse signal; it is a development-time utility with no server-side or content-hosting surface that fraudsters target.

VitePress.history: VitePress is an open-source static site generator built on Vite and Vue, created by the Vite/Vue ecosystem team and released around 2021. It is the successor to VuePress and is used for documentation sites.
VitePress.summary_abuse: VitePress is a static site generator with no notable abuse signal; it is a build tool for generating static documentation sites with no server-side or content-hosting surface.

Vitrin.me.history: Vitrin.me is a Persian-language no-code web app builder targeting Iranian entrepreneurs and SMBs, allowing users to build web applications without writing code. Founded around 2018, it operates primarily in the Iranian market with low pricing tiers.
Vitrin.me.summary_abuse: Vitrin.me is a no-code page builder with minimal public abuse signal. As a platform allowing custom web content creation, it could theoretically host phishing pages, but its niche Persian-language market and low profile suggest no documented abuse patterns. No AUP or abuse reporting details are publicly visible.

Vivenu.history: Vivenu is a German ticketing platform founded in 2019, providing event ticket management and sales tools for event organisers; it has raised venture funding.
Vivenu.summary_abuse: Vivenu is a ticketing platform; ticket platforms can be used to sell fake event tickets, but no documented platform-specific abuse campaigns are known for Vivenu. Its B2B model targeting legitimate event organisers reduces abuse risk.

VIVVO.history: VIVVO is a CMS originally developed in Serbia, primarily used in media and publishing. Limited public information is available about its current development status.
VIVVO.summary_abuse: VIVVO has no notable abuse signal. It is a legacy self-hosted CMS for media publishers with no consumer-facing hosted content features.

Vizium.history: Vizium360 is a US-based healthcare reputation management platform offering patient survey tools, online review generation, and reputation management for medical practices. The platform operates RealPatientRatings.com and has accumulated over 1.5 million verified patient reviews.
Vizium.summary_abuse: Vizium360 is a healthcare-specific review management platform with no meaningful abuse signal. It serves medical providers seeking patient feedback and reputation management, a context that limits fraudulent use to near-zero. The platform's focus on verified patients and healthcare compliance further reduces abuse risk.

Vizury.history: Vizury was founded in 2008 in India by Chetan Kulkarni, Gourav Chindlur, and Vikram Nayak as an ecommerce retargeting and marketing automation platform. In September 2018, Affle India acquired Vizury's commerce business for $10 million; the brand and platform now operate under Affle.
Vizury.summary_abuse: Vizury is an ecommerce marketing automation and retargeting platform with no notable abuse signal. As a B2B tool used by brands in ecommerce and BFSI, it is not a consumer-facing service that fraudsters target. Push notification features could theoretically be misused for spam, but no documented incidents are known.

VK Pixel.history: VK Pixel is the advertising tracking pixel for VKontakte (VK), Russia's largest social network founded in 2006. The pixel tracks user behavior for VK advertising retargeting. VK is owned by VK Group (formerly Mail.ru Group), a Russian technology conglomerate.
VK Pixel.summary_abuse: VK Pixel is the tracking pixel for VKontakte, Russia's largest social network. It collects behavioral data across websites for advertising retargeting. As a Russian company operating under Russian law, it is not GDPR compliant, and data may be accessible to Russian authorities. It carries significant privacy risk for users in EU/Western jurisdictions. VK has been subject to sanctions in some countries.

VKUI.history: VKUI is a React component library developed by VK (VKontakte) and open-sourced around 2019. It provides UI components matching VK's mobile app design language for building VK mini-apps and web interfaces.
VKUI.summary_abuse: VKUI has minimal abuse signal as a UI library. It is developed by VK (Russian social network), and applications built with it may be subject to VK's data policies, but the library itself is not a vehicle for fraud.

Vnda.history: Vnda was founded in 2010 by Denis Osorio and Gustavo Reis in Porto Alegre, Brazil. The company provides an omnichannel ecommerce platform for Brazilian retailers. Vnda was later acquired by Olist, a prominent Brazilian ecommerce group, and now operates as part of the Olist Group's suite of ecommerce solutions.
Vnda.summary_abuse: Vnda is a B2B ecommerce platform serving Brazilian retailers with no notable abuse signal. The platform is used by legitimate businesses to build online stores, and no documented cases of phishing, scam storefronts, or fraud are specifically attributed to Vnda.

VNN Sports.history: VNN (Varsity News Network) was founded in 2010 by Ryan Vaughn and Matt Anderson in Grand Rapids, Michigan, as a school sports marketing and communications platform. In 2020 VNN acquired RallyAroundUs, Washington Prep Athletics Network, and Wisconsin Sports Network. In March 2022 VNN completed a cashless merger with rSchoolToday, combining front-facing products with rSchoolToday's athletic director back-office tools. The combined entity joined PlayOn Sports in May 2023, making VNN a product under the PlayOn Sports umbrella.
VNN Sports.summary_abuse: VNN Sports is a school athletic communications SaaS platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform serves high school athletic departments and has no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing or fraud. No documented abuse cases or security incidents are associated with the service.

Vntana.history: VNTANA was founded in 2012 in Los Angeles by Ashley Crowder and Ben Conway, initially focused on social augmented reality. The company pivoted to 3D asset management and AR for ecommerce, raising $13.2M in total funding including a 2021 round backed by Mark Cuban and former Oculus CEO Brendan Iribe.
Vntana.summary_abuse: VNTANA is a 3D asset management and augmented reality platform for ecommerce and marketing with no notable abuse signal. The platform serves brand and enterprise customers managing digital assets, not a consumer-facing service with significant fraud exposure.

VocalReferences.history: VocalReferences was founded in 2008 and is operated by Local Hits Media LLC, an Israeli startup. The platform collects video and text testimonials from customers, optimizes them for search engines, and embeds them on business websites.
VocalReferences.summary_abuse: VocalReferences is a testimonial and review collection platform with minimal abuse risk. The platform does not allow anonymous content creation and is targeted at SMBs collecting verified customer references. No documented abuse patterns are known.

Voice Intuitive.history: Voice Intuitive is an accessibility platform providing voice and translation tools for web content; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Voice Intuitive.summary_abuse: Voice Intuitive is an accessibility tool with no notable abuse signal. It serves website accessibility needs and has no consumer-facing content hosting surface.

Voizee.history: Voizee is an AI-powered business communication platform launched around 2020, offering SMS marketing, mass texting, virtual phone systems, and website chat widgets for SMBs. Plans range from $25 to $249 per month.
Voizee.summary_abuse: Voizee provides SMS and voice communication services for businesses. Mass texting capabilities could be misused for SMS spam if account verification is weak. However, the platform requires paid subscription and targets SMBs for legitimate customer communication; no specific documented abuse incidents are known.

Volley.history: Volley is a visual website annotation and feedback tool founded around 2018; it is used by teams for collecting visual comments on live websites during design reviews.
Volley.summary_abuse: Volley is a website annotation tool for design feedback with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B collaboration tool with no anonymous consumer content surface.

Voltage.history: Voltage is a Bitcoin Lightning Network payments platform; founding details are not publicly documented.
Voltage.summary_abuse: Voltage is a Bitcoin Lightning Network payment service. Cryptocurrency payment services can be used for illicit transactions including money laundering and payments for illegal goods, though the Lightning Network's public routing somewhat limits anonymity. The platform serves developers and legitimate businesses.

Voltn.history: Voltn is a marketing technology services provider; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Voltn.summary_abuse: Voltn is a marketing technology provider with no notable abuse signal documented.

Volusion.history: Volusion was founded in 1999 in Austin, Texas as one of the early hosted e-commerce platforms. It filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2020 but continued operations and emerged as a restructured company. It experienced a major data breach in 2019 when card-skimming malware was injected via a compromised Google Cloud CDN supply chain affecting thousands of merchant sites.
Volusion.summary_abuse: Volusion is a hosted e-commerce platform that can be used to set up fraudulent storefronts. In 2019 it suffered a significant supply-chain attack where malicious card-skimming JavaScript was served from a compromised subdomain, affecting thousands of merchant checkout pages. This represents a meaningful historical abuse incident at the infrastructure level.

Vonage Video API.history: Vonage Video API (formerly OpenTok by TokBox) was created by TokBox, which was founded around 2010 and pioneered WebRTC-based video communications. TokBox was acquired by Vonage in 2018. Vonage was subsequently acquired by Ericsson in 2022.
Vonage Video API.summary_abuse: Vonage Video API is a WebRTC video communications platform used in legitimate telehealth, education, and collaboration applications. No notable platform-specific abuse patterns are documented; it is a B2B API service with enterprise onboarding requirements.

Vondera.history: Vondera is an Egyptian e-commerce platform founded around 2019 for online store management; it targets merchants in Egypt and the broader MENA region.
Vondera.summary_abuse: Vondera is an Egyptian e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal; like any e-commerce host it could be used to set up fraudulent storefronts, but no documented platform-level abuse patterns are known.

Voog.history: Voog is an Estonian website building platform founded in 2009; it provides multilingual website creation tools and has remained independent.
Voog.summary_abuse: Voog is a website builder with hosted plans; like other SaaS website builders it could be used to host fraudulent pages, but no documented platform-specific abuse campaigns are known.

Voog.com Website Builder.history: Voog.com Website Builder is the website builder product from Voog, an Estonian CMS and e-commerce platform founded in 2009; it is the same underlying company as Voog, providing a user-friendly website and store builder.
Voog.com Website Builder.summary_abuse: Voog.com Website Builder is a hosted website builder with no documented abuse campaigns; like all website builders with hosted plans, fraudulent sites could theoretically be created.

Voomly.history: Voomly is a hosted video course and interactive video platform. ClickFunnels formed Voomly LLC through acquisitions in September 2021, incorporating it into its suite of marketing tools. The platform targets content creators and online course sellers.
Voomly.summary_abuse: Voomly is a video hosting and course delivery platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves content creators and course sellers under ClickFunnels ownership, with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters typically exploit.

Vop.history: Vop is a social commerce integration service for embedding e-commerce into social content; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Vop.summary_abuse: Vop is a social commerce integration tool with no notable abuse signal documented.

Voracio.history: Voracio is a UK cloud SaaS e-commerce platform built on Microsoft .NET, targeting mid-market retailers; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Voracio.summary_abuse: Voracio is a UK e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal; it serves established retailers under enterprise contracts.

Vouched.history: Vouched is a US-based identity verification platform founded around 2018; it provides AI-powered ID and selfie verification services for online platforms and has raised venture funding.
Vouched.summary_abuse: Vouched is an identity verification service designed to prevent fraud and abuse; it has no notable abuse signal as a tool. Its purpose is to verify identities and reduce fake account creation for its clients.

Voucherify.history: Voucherify is a Polish promotion and loyalty engine founded in 2014; it provides APIs for building coupon, referral, and loyalty programs and has raised venture funding.
Voucherify.summary_abuse: Voucherify is a promotions and couponing API. Discount and coupon systems are sometimes exploited through code sharing or coupon abuse (stacking, reuse), but these are merchant-level policy issues rather than platform exploitation.
